programmes.show_description: Old Fossils: Crime caper with the experienced detectives. James Bolam, Amanda Redman and team probe the death of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum.
Scoop: The body of a high-level government official's secretary is found at the bottom of a staircase - and Sister Boniface claims she was dead before the fall.
Smoke and Mirrors: Final Chapter: Despite Mattie's objections, Beth and Cat continue to pursue leads on their own, while the theatre company is forced to change the cast around.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Money For Nothing: When the Sun Hill team form a syndicate to buy a greyhound, Quinnan's Jenny discovers she is part of it.
A Night to Forget: Quinnan's stag night is being organised by Hollis, so will anything go right? The evening certainly promises a few surprises!
A Day to Remember: What could possibly go wrong on Quinnan's wedding day? Garfield's unexpected reappearance is only the beginning!
Judgement Day: Lennox and Rawton are drawn closer together when a dangerous ghost from Rawton's past returns to haunt her.
Haunted: The Sun Hill CID must deal with ghosts, clairvoyants and messages from beyond the grave.
Dr Turner helps a couple whose daughter was born without limbs to have her fitted for prosthetics - but the experience provokes different reactions from her mother and father.
Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading a mother of three to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husband's back.
Feature-length historical drama from the world-famous romantic author, Catherine Cookson. The Christmas of 1913 brings changes for disenchanted young Agnes Conway.
In 1818, Sharpe and Sgt Major Harper accompany a motley crew of soldiers and a headstrong woman through India.
Love and Other Puzzles: When local resident Hillary is found dead, face covered in cold cream next to an unfinished jigsaw, it becomes clear to Boniface a pattern's forming across cases.
Life Licence: Emilia Fox stars in a feature-length forensics mystery. When an ex-convict is killed in his home, the team meet a psychologist who works with murderers.
Nine Lives: James Bolam and Amanda Redman star in the popular crime drama. An elderly lady's body is discovered, partly consumed by her horde of cats. Jack thinks it's murder.
Dr Turner helps a couple whose daughter was born without limbs to have her fitted for prosthetics - but the experience provokes different reactions from her mother and father.
Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading a mother of three to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husband's back.
Noble Rot: After wine critic and connoisseur Raymond Bettane collapses and dies during a tasting, Mick and Shazia find a broken champagne flute and a crushed glass vial.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Breadwinner: Essex humour with the Chigwell sisters. Sharon's cafe is doing well, but Tracey finds her success hard to take. Dorien is in pursuit of a new relationship.
When the Snow Lay Round About: It's two nights before Christmas, and the whole of Sun Hill is desperately hoping there won't be a shout. Pretty unlikely!
New. Life's a Beach: Archie deals with a case of an arrogant medical student's food poisoning, while Jimmi worries that things are moving too quickly with Eva.
New. It's a Sin: Michelle makes Ruth wear a flattering top, but the receptionist knows all too well the consequences if Davey sees her.
Conflict of Interests: Connie and Zubin clash over the treatment of a patient, Donna is uncomfortable about her night with Mickie, and Diane tries to put her life back in order.
What Lies Beneath: Jeff retaliates against unscrupulous gang leader Karl Fontayne. Mads is torn between the values of her old and new lives. Lenny tries not to give his sister bad news.
When the Snow Lay Round About: It's two nights before Christmas, and the whole of Sun Hill is desperately hoping there won't be a shout. Pretty unlikely!
New. Will Janine be able to admit her true feelings before it is too late? Danny's mask begins to slip, and Ronnie struggles through a difficult day.
New. Billie makes a decision that could change his life forever. Janine, meanwhile, attempts to do the right thing, and Ben's worst fears become sickening reality.
The Female of the Species: Robert Hardy stars in the veterinary drama ideal with a cup of tea and a biscuit. Calum becomes more enthusiastic when he meets the new veterinary student.
James reveals the truth about his tragic past to Jan, while Laura and Ken have a disagreement over the commissioning of the trailer-sailer.
Hunger for Bread: Lu infiltrates a local slimming club to discover their secrets, but gets more than she bargained for when she realises her sister is a member.
Thirty Something: Sibling sitcom and the Essex girls have the birthday blues. Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her.
How Not to Cry at Weddings: The world's longest-running sitcom here draws to a poignant end. Everyone is getting ready for the wedding. Clegg wonders if his suit smells of mothballs.
Of Funerals and Fish: The very first episode of the longest running sitcom in the world! When two men remove Compo's television, he retaliates by stealing Nora Batty's bloomers.
Short Back and Palais Glide: It's Blamire, not Foggy, who appears here in the first series of the long-running sitcom. The boys discuss life, love and unpleasant women.
Blood and Water: Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd is sent to the New Zealand town of Brokenwood to investigate the death of a farmer in this opening episode of the drama series.
Nine Lives: James Bolam and Amanda Redman star in the popular crime drama. An elderly lady's body is discovered, partly consumed by her horde of cats. Jack thinks it's murder.
